zh.Castle Cary, Somerset, England: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特卡素 加里
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel於1276年3月19日出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特卡素 加里時，他的父親Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary27歲，母親Alianore De Soules17歲。他於大約1294年與LOVEL Mrs在英國戴文莫德波利結婚。他去世於1351年1月31日在英國薩默塞特諾斯 卡德波利享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel于1276年3月19日出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特卡素 加里时，他的父亲Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary27岁，母亲Alianore De Soules17岁。他于大约1294年与LOVEL Mrs在英国戴文莫德波利结婚。他去世于1351年1月31日在英国薩默塞特諾斯 卡德波利享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lovel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Lovell
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lovel ‘wolf cub’, a diminutive of lou, love ‘wolf’. Compare Love and Low .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel was born on 19 March 1276, in Castle Cary, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary, was 27 and his mother, Alianore De Soules, was 17. He married LOVEL Mrs about 1294, in Modbury, Devon, England. He died on 31 January 1351, in North Cadbury, Somerset, England, at the age of 74.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel est né le 19 mars 1276, à Castle Cary, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Alianore De Soules, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé LOVEL Mrs environ 1294, à Modbury, Devon, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 31 janvier 1351, à North Cadbury, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 74 ans.

März 1276 in Castle Cary, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary 27 und seine Mutter Alianore De Soules 17. Er hat um 1294 in Modbury, Devon, England LOVEL Mrs geheiratet. Er starb am 31. Januar 1351 im Alter von 74 Jahren in North Cadbury, Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel è nato il il 19 marzo 1276 nel Castle Cary, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Alianore De Soules, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato LOVEL Mrs circa 1294, nel Modbury, Devon, England. Morì il 31 gennaio 1351, nel North Cadbury, Somerset, England, all'età di 74 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovelが1276年3月19日にイギリスイギリスソメルセトカストル カーイで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Caryは27歳，母Alianore De Soulesは17歳であった。約1294年にイギリスデボンモドブリーでLOVEL Mrsと結婚した。彼は1351年1月31日にイギリスソメルセトノース カドブリーで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel(이)가 1276년 3월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 카스틀 카리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Alianore De Soules(은)는 17세였다. 그는 대략 1294년 영국 데폰 모드버이에서 LOVEL Mrs(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1351년 1월 31일 영국 서머세트 노르스 카드버이에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel nasceu em 19 de março de 1276, em Castle Cary, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Alianore De Soules, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com LOVEL Mrs aproximadamente 1294, em Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 31 de janeiro de 1351, em North Cadbury, Somerset, Inglaterra, com 74 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel родился 19 марта 1276 г. в местности кастл кэри, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Alianore De Soules, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени LOVEL Mrs около 1294 г. в местности модбури, девон, Англия. Он умер 31 января 1351 г. в местности норт кадбури, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 74 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Lovel 1st Baron Lovel nació el 19 de marzo de 1276, en Castle Cary, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Hugh Lovel Lord of Cary, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Alianore De Soules, tenía 17 años. Se casó con LOVEL Mrs aproximadamente 1294, en Modbury, Devon, Inglaterra. Murió el 31 de enero de 1351, en North Cadbury, Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 74 años.
